title: Binary, hex, casting and the modulo operation
_comment: See decks/W1C2.json for the format and the reasoning behind it.
Reconstructed from the retired LCS deck W1C3Digitalpracticeconv.pdf.
Two of that deck's slides (the 8-bit binary addition drills on p9/p10 and
the 2's complement subtraction on p15) have no book counterpart - they are
worked by hand, not in a coding window. They are carried here as 'prompt'
slides rather than pushed into the book, since they only make sense live.

slides.title: Practice: binary addition
slides.body: Add the decimal numbers 177 and 23 in 8-bit binary.

slides.title: Now try this one
slides.note: Recall that 177 is 10110001. Does your result make sense?

slides.title: Practice: 2's complement
slides.body: Calculate 110 − 77 in binary 2's complement — that is, perform the addition 110 + (−77).

slides.title: What we covered today
items: Three ways to read a binary number: unsigned, signed magnitude, 2's complement
Casting between data types: ave = ((float) a)/((float) b);
